reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that a group of order 9 is abelian — i.e., the given group's multiplication is commutative. The corrected formalization correctly expresses this as `∀ a b : G, a * b = b * a`, a property of the existing [Group G]'s mul. The original uses `instance : CommGroup G`, which constructs a new CommGroup typeclass instance. CommGroup extends Group and CommMonoid, so this creates a new algebraic structure that includes its own `mul` field. While in practice the 'intended' proof would reuse the existing group operations, the Lean type `CommGroup G` does not enforce agreement with the existing [Group G]. This makes the original weaker in the sense that it only proves some commutative group structure exists on G (trivially true for any type with the right cardinality), rather than proving the specific given group is commutative. This is the same pattern mistake as proofnet-85.
